title: Kantyna Nova website photo grade: batch-retouch 8 raw restaurant food photos to a cohesive premium look + desktop/mobile crops

source.reference: Professional Photo Editor for Restaurant Brand

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief ("Professional Photo Editor for Restaurant Brand"): "I am looking for a professional photo editor and retoucher to improve the quality of photos for a new website for our restaurant and catering brand"; scope includes "Professional enhancement of food and restaurant photos", "Improving overall image quality, sharpness, colors, lighting, and contrast", "Consistent color grading across all materials", "Cropping and adjusting image composition for modern website layouts", "Preparing photos for desktop and mobile website use", "Retouching imperfections while maintaining a natural look", and "Creating a cohesive visual style for the entire website". The real client is the Polish catering brand "Nasza Kantyna" — replaced with the IP-clean fictional brand "Kantyna Nova" and a fictional owner persona (Marta Zawadzka). REASONABLY ENRICHED into a runnable Adobe-only edit benchmark: the from-scratch website build is out of connector scope, so the task is scoped to exactly the deliverable the brief rewards — grading a set of raw, dull, tilted restaurant food photos to one cohesive premium look and cutting matched desktop + mobile crops. "Retouching imperfections while maintaining a natural look" is honored via whole-image tone/color grading and whole-frame background lens-blur (NOT inpainting/object-removal, which is banned); no generative fill, no upscale, no from-scratch art.
one_line_ask: Batch-retouch eight raw restaurant food photos to one cohesive premium color grade, straightened and cleaned up, then deliver desktop (16:9) and mobile (4:5) crops of each plus a review board for owner sign-off.
full_brief: Kantyna Nova, a modern Polish comfort-food restaurant and catering brand, is launching a new website and needs its food photography brought up to a premium, cohesive standard. The owner, Marta Zawadzka, has handed off eight raw phone-and-DSLR shots straight from a shoot day: two hero plates (a bowl of pierogi with browned onions, a golden schnitzel platter), a bright beet-and-goat-cheese salad, a hearty bowl of zurek soup, a slice of layered sernik cheesecake, a flat-lay of a catering grazing spread, a bar shot of a craft kompot drink, and a wide interior shot of the dining room. As-shot they are inconsistent and unappetizing: several are slightly tilted, most are flat and underexposed with a cool/greenish canteen-lighting cast, backgrounds are busy, and the colours drift from photo to photo so they would never read as one brand. The job is a pure retouch/grade edit (no layout, no new imagery): (1) straighten anything tilted and auto-tone every shot to a clean baseline; (2) establish ONE cohesive premium look on the lead hero — warm the white balance off the canteen green, lift the shadows, push vibrance so food reads rich and appetizing, boost contrast, and settle on a reusable food-grade preset so the whole set can be aligned to one named look; (3) apply that same preset across all remaining shots so the full set looks graded together, with per-shot exposure nudges where a frame is darker; (4) throw the busiest backgrounds gently out of focus with a subject-aware background depth-of-field lens blur (keeps the plate/glass sharp, softens the clutter behind it) so the food/subject reads as hero while keeping a natural look; (5) crop-and-resize each graded image into TWO matched deliverables — a desktop hero crop at 16:9 (1920x1080) and a mobile crop at 4:5 (1080x1350) — so the site has correctly-composed assets for both breakpoints without stretching. Keep everything natural — no plastic over-processing. Deliverables: sixteen graded, correctly-cropped JPEGs (8 photos x desktop + mobile), organised in a clean folder structure, plus a Firefly review board deep-link assembling the graded set for Marta to approve the look in one click. Brand direction: warm, rich, premium, appetizing; consistent grade across every image; natural, not garish.

inputs.name: raw_hero_pierogi.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal candid shoot-day snapshot of a rustic bowl of Polish pierogi topped with browned butter onions and fresh dill, on a dark ceramic plate on a wooden restaurant table. Slightly tilted horizon (~3 degrees), flat underexposed lighting, cool greenish canteen white balance, busy background of cutlery and a water glass slightly out of frame. Real steam, dough texture, no studio gloss, documentary feel.
inputs.realism_notes: Lead hero the cohesive look is built on; deliberately tilted, flat, cool/green so the grade chain (straighten, auto-tone, warm, lift shadows, vibrance, contrast) has real work.

inputs.name: raw_hero_schnitzel.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal candid snapshot of a golden breaded schnitzel platter with a lemon wedge and parsley potatoes on a white plate, on a canteen table. Dull flat overhead lighting, cool cast, mild underexposure, cluttered background of napkins and a menu card. Real crumb texture, grease sheen, no CGI gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Second hero; same imperfections so the shared preset can be validated across two hero plates, plus a busy background for the lens-blur step.

inputs.name: raw_salad_beet_goatcheese.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal snapshot of a beet and goat-cheese salad with walnuts and greens on a slate board, harsh cool overhead light, flat greenish white balance, busy table edge with a fork and crumbs. Real produce texture, no studio polish.
inputs.realism_notes: Colour-heavy subject that exposes any grade inconsistency; justifies vibrance and warm WB in the shared look.

inputs.name: raw_soup_zurek.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal snapshot of a bowl of Polish zurek sour rye soup with sliced sausage and egg in a bread bowl, dim warm restaurant light but muddy and underexposed, slightly cool cast, cluttered background. Real steam and broth surface, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Darkest frame; justifies a per-shot exposure nudge on top of the shared preset.

inputs.name: raw_dessert_sernik.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal snapshot of a slice of layered Polish sernik cheesecake with a berry coulis on a small plate, flat dull cafe lighting, cool white balance, distracting bright window blown out behind it. Real crumb and cream texture, fork mark, no gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Dessert hero; flat/cool tone justifies the same warm premium grade and dual crop.

inputs.name: raw_catering_grazing_flatlay.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal overhead flat-lay of a catering grazing spread: cured meats, cheeses, olives, grapes, bread, arranged on a large board, shot under mixed cool canteen light, slightly tilted, flat and busy. Real food texture, documentary shoot-day feel, no studio styling.
inputs.realism_notes: Catering-focus shot (the brand is a catering brand); tilt + busy composition justify straighten and both crops.

inputs.name: raw_drink_kompot.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal snapshot of a tall glass of ruby-red Polish kompot fruit drink with condensation and fruit pieces, on a bar counter, dim muddy warm-but-flat light, busy shelf of bottles blurred behind. Real glass reflections and droplets, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Beverage hero; muddy/flat tone justifies grade, and the busy shelf justifies whole-frame background lens-blur.

inputs.name: raw_interior_dining_room.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal wide snapshot of a cozy modern Polish restaurant dining room with wooden tables, warm pendant lights and a bar, shot handheld with a slightly tilted horizon (~2 degrees), flat and slightly cool exposure, empty of people. Real ambient interior, documentary feel, no HDR.
inputs.realism_notes: Ambience shot for the site header; tilt + flat exposure justify straighten, auto-tone and the wide desktop crop.

inputs.name: kantyna_nova_brand_direction.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a short one-page brand photo direction note for a modern Polish comfort-food restaurant and catering brand named 'Kantyna Nova', owner Marta Zawadzka. Specify the desired look: warm and rich white balance (correct the cool canteen-green cast), lifted shadows, elevated vibrance for appetizing food, gentle contrast, natural not over-processed; consistent grade across all images; desktop hero crops at 16:9 (1920x1080) and mobile crops at 4:5 (1080x1350). Plain text.
inputs.realism_notes: The client's written look/spec brief so the grade target and crop dimensions are grounded, not invented by the editor.

outputs.name: desktop_crops_16x9_x8
outputs.spec: Eight graded, straightened, cohesively-color-graded desktop hero JPEGs cropped/resized to 16:9 at 1920x1080 (pierogi, schnitzel, salad, zurek, sernik, grazing flat-lay, kompot, interior), all sharing one warm premium look.

outputs.name: mobile_crops_4x5_x8
outputs.spec: Eight matched mobile JPEGs of the same graded images cropped/resized to 4:5 at 1080x1350 for the mobile website breakpoint, subject-centered and consistent with the desktop set.

outputs.name: kantyna_nova_look_preset
outputs.spec: The reusable food-grade preset settled on from the lead hero (warm WB + lifted shadows + vibrance + contrast) and applied across the set to enforce a cohesive look.

outputs.name: Kantyna_Nova_web_photos_folder
outputs.spec: Clean Creative Cloud folder structure (desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders) holding all sixteen deliverable JPEGs, uploaded and organised for hand-off.

outputs.name: Kantyna_Nova_review_board
outputs.spec: Firefly board deep-link assembling the eight graded desktop crops for Marta to approve the cohesive look in one click.

connector_workflow.note: Create the clean hand-off folder structure (Kantyna_Nova_web_photos with desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders) up front.
Level the ~3-degree tilt on the lead hero pierogi shot before establishing the look.
Auto-tone the hero to a clean exposure/contrast baseline.
Warm the white balance off the cool greenish canteen cast toward the premium warm look.
Lift the crushed shadows so the food reads rich without going flat.
Push vibrance so the onions/dill/dough read appetizing while keeping it natural.
Add gentle contrast to finish the cohesive premium grade; this frame is the look reference.
List the available food/warm presets and settle on the named 'kantyna_nova_look' preset that best matches the reference grade, to reuse across the set for consistency.
Cut the desktop hero crop of the graded pierogi at 16:9 / 1920x1080.
Cut the matched mobile crop of the graded pierogi at 4:5 / 1080x1350, subject-centered.
Apply the shared preset to the schnitzel hero so it matches the lead grade in one step.
Gently throw the cluttered napkin/menu background out of focus so the plate reads as hero (whole-frame).
Desktop 16:9 / 1920x1080 crop of the graded schnitzel.
Matched mobile 4:5 / 1080x1350 crop of the graded schnitzel.
Apply the shared preset to the beet/goat-cheese salad for a consistent look.
Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded salad.
Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded salad.
Apply the shared preset to the zurek soup shot.
Per-shot exposure nudge on the darkest frame so it still matches the graded set.
Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded, exposure-corrected zurek.
Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded zurek.
Apply the shared preset to the sernik cheesecake shot.
Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded sernik.
Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded sernik.
Level the tilted grazing flat-lay before grading.
Apply the shared preset to the straightened catering grazing spread.
Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded grazing flat-lay.
Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded grazing flat-lay.
Apply the shared preset to the kompot drink shot.
Gently blur the busy bar-shelf background so the glass reads as hero (whole-frame).
Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded kompot.
Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded kompot.
Level the ~2-degree tilt on the wide dining-room ambience shot.
Apply the shared preset to the interior so the ambience shot matches the food set.
Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded interior for the site header.
Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded interior.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: pierogi_desktop_16x9, pierogi_mobile_4x5, schnitzel_desktop_16x9, schnitzel_mobile_4x5, salad_desktop_16x9, salad_mobile_4x5, zurek_desktop_16x9, zurek_mobile_4x5, sernik_desktop_16x9, sernik_mobile_4x5, grazing_desktop_16x9, grazing_mobile_4x5, kompot_desktop_16x9, kompot_mobile_4x5, interior_desktop_16x9, interior_mobile_4x5
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for all sixteen deliverable crops into the prepared folder structure.
Finalize the upload, filing the desktop/mobile JPEGs into their desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders for hand-off.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: pierogi_desktop_16x9, schnitzel_desktop_16x9, salad_desktop_16x9, zurek_desktop_16x9, sernik_desktop_16x9, grazing_desktop_16x9, kompot_desktop_16x9, interior_desktop_16x9
connector_workflow.note: Assemble the eight graded desktop crops onto a Firefly board deep-link so Marta can approve the cohesive look in one click.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining throughout. The lead hero builds the shared look as an 8-link pipeline: raw_hero_pierogi.jpg -> auto_straighten(2) -> auto_tone(3) -> color_temperature(4) -> dark_portions(5) -> vibrance(6) -> brightness_contrast(7) -> list_presets(8, settles on the named kantyna_nova_look preset that matches the reference grade) -> desktop crop(9) + mobile crop(10), each step consuming only the prior step's output. That preset(8) then fans out as a shared input into every other photo's grade step: schnitzel(11), salad(15), zurek(18), sernik(22), grazing(26), kompot(29), interior(34) each pair the preset with their (optionally straightened) raw frame, then chain into optional lens_blur / exposure fixes and their own desktop+mobile crop pair. All sixteen crop outputs converge into the upload session(37)->finalize(38, also consuming the folder structure from step 1) to file into desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders; the eight graded desktop crops converge into the Firefly review board(39). Only the four raw straighten/grade seeds and step-1 folder creation originate from supplied inputs; every deliverable emerges from the assembled chain and the single shared preset.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship edit: 39 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across two groups (image_* raster grading/cropping, asset_* folders/upload) plus create_firefly_board -- far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty is genuine batch-consistency work: build one premium look on a lead hero via a six-step manual grade, settle on a reusable named preset, then enforce that exact look across seven more heterogeneous subjects (plates, salad, soup, dessert, flat-lay, drink, interior) with per-shot straighten/exposure/lens-blur corrections, and cut a matched desktop-16:9 + mobile-4:5 crop pair for all eight, ending in organised upload and a review board. It respects every hard limit: pure edit of supplied photos, no generative fill/expand/reframe-crop, no text-to-image or from-scratch art, no upscaling, no object-removal/inpainting (imperfections are handled by whole-image tone/color grade + whole-frame lens blur, not reconstruction), image_fill_area unused, no InDesign/layout composition. lens_blur is whole-image with no masked-DOF claim.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo; no upscaling/super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction/inpainting; no bespoke font design; no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is not used at all.
reverify.notes: Every step stays in bounds and uses real Adobe-connector behavior. (a) 'Retouching imperfections while maintaining a natural look' from the source brief is satisfied by whole-image tone/color/exposure grading (image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_dark_portions, image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation, image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast, image_adjust_exposure) plus a subject-aware background lens blur, NOT by inpainting or object removal (no reconstruction of occluded pixels). (b) image_apply_lens_blur (n12, n30) is used per its real behavior: a subject-aware background depth-of-field blur that keeps the subject (plate/glass) sharp while softening the busy background — exactly the 'make the food read as hero' intent; no manual mask is authored or claimed. (c) image_list_presets (n8) enumerates the available built-in presets and selects the named food/warm preset that matches the reference grade, and image_apply_preset (n11,15,18,22,26,29,34) re-applies that same named preset for cohesion — the honest, in-palette way to enforce consistency (no custom-preset-authoring capability is claimed). (d) All crops use image_crop_and_resize to reframe existing pixels to 16:9/4:5 at fixed pixel sizes — pure cropping/downscaling to target dimensions, never upscaling beyond source. (e) No layout/InDesign, no Express-widget or Canva tools, no video/audio. create_firefly_board (n39) only assembles CC assets the chain itself produced (honest moodboard use). All 39 steps server=='adobe'; 15 distinct Adobe tools (>=6, and tools_used lists all 15 including create_firefly_board); 39 calls (>=10) — qualifies as long-horizon flagship edit. Slot-match confirmed: category food_bev_photo, operation O1 / family Photo & Image, output_modality image, workflow_nature edit, vertical food_beverage; grounded in the 'Professional Photo Editor for Restaurant Brand' candidate; IP-clean fictional brand Kantyna Nova / persona Marta Zawadzka.
